reply:
[[BLOCK:srt_73]]
Speaker 2: You know, when Covid (Covid) first broke out in the United States, many universities rushed to launch online degree programs.

[[BLOCK:srt_74]]
Speaker 2: But we already had well-established online degree programs in place at that time.

[[BLOCK:srt_75]]
Speaker 2: So the real challenge wasn’t starting from scratch—it was figuring out how to make our existing online programs even better, continually refining and upgrading them with the latest technology.

[[BLOCK:srt_76]]
Speaker 3: So how does Golden Gate University’s online education help students balance their career development?

[[BLOCK:srt_77]]
Speaker 3: And as for the dean, sometimes he deliberately looks at the camera a little longer—after all, he’s not usually looking directly at you, right?

[[BLOCK:srt_78]]
Speaker 3: Yes, every now and then his gaze drifts slightly. Have you noticed that?

[[BLOCK:srt_79]]
Speaker 3: Right, right—he’s really making an effort to keep his eyes on the camera, isn’t he?

[[BLOCK:srt_80]]
Speaker 3: Exactly—otherwise when he watches the footage later—

[[BLOCK:srt_81]]
Speaker 3: All right, please go ahead—

[[BLOCK:srt_82]]
Speaker 2: Right, I just—

[[BLOCK:srt_83]]
Speaker 2: I was looking at the wrong camera just now.

[[BLOCK:srt_84]]
Speaker 1: No worries—just, yes, look over there.
